---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2025-12-14"
---

```{r setup}

# To clear your environment 
remove(list = ls())

# load common packages
library(here)
here()
source(here('code', 'func_packages.R'))

library(sf)


# Find where proj.db lives (usually inside R's rgdal/terra installation)
proj_path <- dirname(Sys.which("gdal_translate"))  # or search manually
Sys.setenv(PROJ_LIB = "C:/path/to/proj/share/proj")  # set before loading terra/sf


g.jl <- 'G:/My Drive/NatCap/projects/KCL_Welcome/jlg-london/tree_planting'

g.aoi <- "G:/Shared drives/Wellcome Trust Project Data/1_preprocess/UrbanCoolingModel/OfficialWorkingInputs/AOIs"

g.invest.out <- 'G:/Shared drives/Wellcome Trust Project Data/2_postprocess_intermediate/UCM_official_runs/camden_JL_runs/intermediate/'


```





```{r aoi data}

bor_i <- 'Camden'



## aoi at borough level 
f <- file.path(g.aoi, 'London_Borough_aoi.shp')
aoi.bor_i <- st_read(f, quiet = T) %>% select(NAME, GSS_CODE) %>%
  filter(NAME == bor_i)


## aoi at lsoa level 
f_aoi <- file.path(g.aoi, 'GLA_Tree_Cover_by_LSOA.gpkg')
aoi.lsoa <- st_read(f_aoi, quiet = T) %>%
  rename('geoid' = 'id', 'TCC' = 'tree_pct') %>%
  mutate(geoid = as.character(geoid)) 

aoi.lsoa_i <- aoi.lsoa %>%
  filter(Neighborhood_name == bor_i)


f <- file.path(g.aoi, paste0('GLA_Tree_Cover_by_LSOA_', bor_i, '.gpkg') ); f
st_write(aoi.lsoa_i, dsn = f, layer = bor_i, delete_layer = T, delete_dsn = T)
```



## zonal stats


```{r test run }

library(terra)
library(sf)
library(exactextractr)
library(dplyr)

# ---------------------------
# 1. Read data
# ---------------------------
f.r <- 'T_air_camden_scenario0_22deg_2uhi.tif'
# f.r <- 'T_air_camden_scenario7_22deg_2uhi.tif'
r   <- rast(paste0(g.invest.out, f.r))

snr_text <- gsub('T_air_camden_|_22deg_2uhi.tif', '', f.r); snr_text

aoi <- aoi.lsoa_i

crs(r) <- crs(vect(aoi))   # fix broken raster CRS

# ---------------------------
# 2. Zonal stats — entire AOI in one call
# ---------------------------
aoi$mean_T_air <- exact_extract(r, aoi, 'mean')

# Multiple stats at once if needed:
# stats <- exact_extract(r, aoi, c('mean', 'median', 'stdev', 'min', 'max'))
# aoi   <- bind_cols(aoi, stats)

# ---------------------------
# 3. City-level aggregation
# ---------------------------
city_summary <- aoi %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(Neighborhood_name) %>%
  summarise(across(mean_T_air, list(mean=mean, min=min, max=max, sd=sd), na.rm=TRUE))

# ---------------------------
# 4. Save
# ---------------------------
# f_out <- paste0(g.jl, '/', 'T_air_mean_lsoa_', bor_i, '_', snr_text, '.gpkg')
# st_write(aoi, f_out, layer = "T_air_mean_lsoa", delete_layer = TRUE)


# ---------------------------
# 4. Visualize
# ---------------------------

# 4a. Map: spatial distribution of mean T_air per LSOA
p <- 
  aoi %>%
  ggplot(.) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = mean_T_air), colour = NA) +
  facet_wrap(~Neighborhood_name) +            # one panel per city
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Mean Air Temperature by LSOA", subtitle = snr_text)


library(scico)      # perceptually uniform, colorblind-safe
library(colorspace) # diverging palettes

# ---------------------------
# OPTION 4: Built-in — viridis "turbo" for max contrast across range
# ---------------------------
p + scale_fill_distiller(palette = "YlOrBr", direction = 1)

```



```{r batch }

library(terra)
library(sf)
library(exactextractr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scico)
library(purrr)

# ---------------------------
# 1. Find all matching rasters
# ---------------------------
raster_files <- list.files(
  g.invest.out,
  pattern = "^T_air_camden_scenario.*_22deg_2uhi\\.tif$",
  full.names = TRUE
)

# ---------------------------
# 2. First pass: compute stats for all scenarios
#    and determine global T_air range for shared scale
# ---------------------------
aoi_base <- aoi.lsoa_i   # base AOI (no mean_T_air yet)

all_results <- map(raster_files, function(f) {
  
  snr_text <- gsub("T_air_camden_|_22deg_2uhi\\.tif", "", basename(f))
  message("Processing: ", snr_text)
  
  r <- rast(f)
  crs(r) <- crs(vect(aoi_base))    # fix broken raster CRS
  
  aoi_i <- aoi_base
  aoi_i$mean_T_air <- exact_extract(r, aoi_i, "mean")
  aoi_i$scenario   <- snr_text
  
  # Save per-scenario gpkg
  f_out <- file.path(g.jl, paste0("T_air_mean_lsoa_", bor_i, "_", snr_text, ".gpkg"))
  st_write(aoi_i, f_out, layer = "T_air_mean_lsoa", delete_layer = TRUE, quiet = TRUE)
  
  return(aoi_i)
  
}) %>% bind_rows()

```


```{r plot maps}
# ---------------------------
# 3. Global color scale (same across all maps)
# ---------------------------
t_range <- range(all_results$mean_T_air, na.rm = TRUE)

# ---------------------------
# 4. Plot all scenarios on shared scale
# ---------------------------
p_all <- all_results %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = mean_T_air), colour = "white", linewidth = 0.05) +
  facet_wrap(~scenario, ncol = 3) +
  # scale_fill_scico(
  #   palette  = "roma",
  #   limits   = t_range,            # ← shared scale key line
  #   name     = "T_air (°C)",
  #   breaks   = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 5)
  # ) +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "YlOrBr", 
                       name = "T_air (°C)", 
                       type = 'seq',
                       direction = 1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    legend.key.width = unit(2, "cm"), 
    panel.grid       = element_blank(),
    axis.text        = element_blank(),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = paste("Mean Air Temperature by LSOA —", bor_i),
    subtitle = "All scenarios, shared colour scale"
  )

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(g.jl, 'figures', paste0("T_air_mean_lsoa_", bor_i, "_all_scenarios.png")),
  plot     = p_all,
  width    = 7, height = ceiling(length(raster_files) / 3) * 3,
  units    = "in", dpi = 300
)

```



```{r sumamry table + plot}


library(openxlsx)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(forcats)

# ---------------------------
# 1. Build summary table
# ---------------------------
summary_tbl <- all_results %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(scenario) %>%
  summarise(
    n_lsoa  = n(),
    mean_T  = round(mean(mean_T_air, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    min_T   = round(min(mean_T_air,  na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    max_T   = round(max(mean_T_air,  na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    sd_T    = round(sd(mean_T_air,   na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    range_T = round(max(mean_T_air,  na.rm = TRUE) -
                    min(mean_T_air,  na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  ) %>%
  arrange(scenario)

# ---------------------------
# 2. Save as formatted xlsx
# ---------------------------

f_xlsx <- file.path(g.jl, paste0("T_air_summary_", bor_i, ".xlsx"))
writexl::write_xlsx(x = summary_tbl, path = f_xlsx)
message("Saved: ", f_xlsx)



# ---------------------------
# 3. Plot summary table as figure
# ---------------------------

# 3a. Dot-range plot: mean ± sd, with min-max range
p_summary <- summary_tbl %>%
  mutate(scenario = fct_reorder(scenario, mean_T)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = scenario)) +
  # min–max range bar
  geom_linerange(aes(xmin = min_T, xmax = max_T),
                 linewidth = 3, colour = "#BDC3C7") +
  # ±1 SD bar
  geom_linerange(aes(xmin = mean_T - sd_T, xmax = mean_T + sd_T),
                 linewidth = 3, colour = "#2980B9", alpha = 0.7) +
  # mean point
  geom_point(aes(x = mean_T), size = 4, colour = "#C0392B") +
  # mean label
  geom_text(aes(x = mean_T, label = sprintf("%.3f", mean_T)),
            vjust = -1, size = 3, colour = "#C0392B", fontface = "bold") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    x        = "Mean T_air (°C)",
    y        = NULL,
    title    = paste("Air Temperature by Scenario —", bor_i),
    subtitle = "Red dot = mean  |  Blue bar = ±1 SD  |  Grey bar = min–max range"
  )


# ---------------------------
# 4. Save figures
# ---------------------------
ggsave(file.path(g.jl, 'figures', paste0("T_air_summary_dotrange_", bor_i, ".png")),
       plot = p_summary, width = 7, height = 0.5 * nrow(summary_tbl) + 2,
       units = "in", dpi = 300)

```
